199.code: printf("%d\n\n",num[0]);//最后栈中只剩下一个结果数据
199.reason: [Using %d to print a double value leads to undefined behavior; should cast to int or use correct format specifier. Also, problem requires integer division but cal uses double division.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  Format specifier '%d' expects 'int', but argument 'num[0]' is 'double'. This type mismatch causes a compilation error/warning and undefined behavior at runtime.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  Format specifier '%d' expects argument of type 'int', but argument 2 has type 'double', causing compilation error
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Using %d to print a double value leads to undefined behavior; should cast to int or use correct format specifier. Also, problem requires integer division but cal uses double division.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Using %d to print a double value leads to undefined behavior; should cast to int or use correct format specifier]

79.reason: [scanf returns the number of successfully read items, not EOF on failure in this context; condition should check for != 1 or != EOF properly. Also, infinite loop if input ends without '='.]
